title: Residential Substance Use Disorder Treatment Facilities * Men - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Mental & Behavioral Health > Residential Substance Use Disorder Treatment Facilities * Men 7 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - No Filter Adolescents Deafness Fathers Men Offender/Ex-Offender Issues Women Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF BLANCHET HOUSE OF HOSPITALITY BLANCHET HOUSE RESIDENTIAL PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a 7-9 month residential living program with case management services. Residents start with in-house work programs and progress to securing stable, independent employment. Residents must maintain sobriety for the duration of the program. 310 NW Glisan Street Portland, OR 97209 Eligibility: Men age 18 and older who are in recovery from substance abuse issues. Individuals with convictions for arson or sex offenses are not allowed into the program. Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (971) 337-8747 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CITYTEAM RECOVERY PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers long-term (average of 6 months) residential substance use disorder treatment to men struggling with addiction. Includes individual case management, recovery-based classes, community involvement, and other services. Also offers up to 24 months of optional additional residential-housing after-care. 526 SE Grand Avenue Portland, OR 97214 Eligibility: Men age 18 and older who are struggling with addiction. Low-barrier program. Barriers preventing access to services include: possessing an SO, needing medically assisted treatment, needing a dual-diagnosis facility. Hours: Intake: Monday-Friday 9am-4pm Facility: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (503) 318-7794 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CODA BEAVERTON RECOVERY CENTER MEN'S RESIDENTIAL TREATMENT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides gender-specific residential treatment. Primary goals include recovery, self-management, obtaining employment and housing, building a support network, and helping individuals not to relapse into a previous bad habit or criminal behavior. Access to methadone services available to program participants. 17911 NW Evergreen Place Beaverton, OR 97006 Eligibility: Men age 18 and older; accepts patients with cases in child welfare or criminal justice systems Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (855) 733-2632 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CODA GRESHAM RECOVERY CENTER WOMEN’S AND MEN’S RESIDENTIAL TREATMENT MEN'S RESIDENTIAL TREATMENT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides gender-specific residential treatment. Primary goals include recovery, self-management, obtaining employment and housing, building a support network, and helping individuals not to relapse into a previous bad habit or criminal behavior. Access to methadone services available to program participants. 1427 SE 182nd Avenue Portland, OR 97233 Eligibility: Men age 18 and older; accepts patients with cases in child welfare or criminal justice systems Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week (503) 761-6006 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CODA TIGARD RECOVERY CENTER MEN'S RESIDENTIAL MEN'S RESIDENTIAL TREATMENT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides gender-specific residential treatment. Primary goals include recovery, self-management, obtaining employment and housing, building a support network, and helping individuals not to relapse into a previous bad habit or criminal behavior. Access to methadone services available to program participants. 10362 SW McDonald Street Tigard, OR 97224 Eligibility: Men age 18 and older; accepts patients with cases in child welfare or criminal justice systems Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week (503) 624-8304 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PORTLAND RESCUE MISSION PORTLAND RESCUE MISSION THE HARBOR THE HARBOR Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides faith-based residential substance abuse treatment. The residential program can accommodate up to 38 men in a retreat-like setting. Residential programs begin with a 5-week assessment, stabilization, and relapse prevention program. On completion, graduates are eligible to enter a one-year holistic recovery program. Transition programs are available upon completion of a one-year holistic recovery program. 10336 NE Wygant Street Portland, OR 97220 Eligibility: Men age 18 and older Hours: Phone hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Walk-in hours: Tuesday/Thursday 11am-1pm: Bunside Center: 111 W Burnside Street, Portland, Oregon, 97209 Email (503) 746-9664 Get Directions Visit Website More Details UNION GOSPEL MISSION LIFECHANGE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF This faith-based program is an addiction recovery community providing a long-term residential drug and alcohol recovery program for people who are willing to make a long-term commitment. Goal-oriented recovery program, not time-based. Program provides for all personal needs, food, clothing, housing, and hygiene products. Requirements include work, participation in religious activities and educational programs. 3 NW Third Avenue Portland, OR 97209 Eligibility: Men and women (including women with children) Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (503) 274-4483 629 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BLANCHET HOUSE OF HOSPITALITY BLANCHET HOUSE RESIDENTIAL PROGRAM CITYTEAM RECOVERY PROGRAM CODA MEN'S RESIDENTIAL TREATMENT CODA MEN'S RESIDENTIAL TREATMENT CODA MEN'S RESIDENTIAL TREATMENT PORTLAND RESCUE MISSION THE HARBOR UNION GOSPEL MISSION LIFECHANGE
